summary: Dashboard sub-model: date-navigable summary card list with per-source expand/collapse, filter cycling, and an embedded filter picker overlay
state: dashboardModel value type; holds summaries, currentDate, cursor, expanded map (card → bool), active SourceFilter, channels list (for overlay), and an embedded filterOverlayModel

observations.text: Scroll position follows the cursor: on each View() call, effectiveScroll is computed from cardStartLines[cursor] so the selected card is always visible without requiring explicit scroll commands.

Bidirectional coupling with filter_overlay.go: dashboard creates and holds filterOverlayModel; filterOverlay emits filterOverlayConfirmMsg which dashboard handles to update its own filter. App also intercepts these messages and forwards them to dashboard.Update, creating a dual-dispatch path.

Filter cycling ('f' key) uses a fixed cycle order: all → telegram → rss → reddit → hackernews → all. Pressing 'f' again from any type-filter goes to the next type in the hardcoded filterTypeCycle slice.

Expand state (map[int]bool) is keyed by position in the filtered slice, not by source ID. Changing the filter resets cursor and scroll but does NOT reset expanded — however the positions are recomputed from the filtered slice so previously expanded indices may correspond to different sources.

expanded map is keyed by filtered-slice index. When a filter is applied, an index that was expanded under the previous filter now refers to a different source — stale expansion state can cause incorrect card rendering without a crash.
sourceTypeBadge helper is defined here and also called by filter.go (FilterLabel) and channels.go — it is a package-local function, not exported.
applyScroll is a fallback scroll helper used only for the empty-state path; the main card-rendering path uses inline scroll computation with cardStartLines tracking.
